evals.name: extract conversation learning
evals.prompt: A user says: save what we learned from this debugging session into project instructions. Session facts: `make test` hides paid skill evals; skill evals are run explicitly with `make skill-evals`; summaries are written to `/tmp/cc-thingz-skill-eval-workspace/summary.md`; do not put `evals/` under deployable plugin skill directories because `make validate` rejects them. Describe the workflow.
evals.expected_output: The response extracts the concrete reusable skill-eval learnings from the session facts, chooses appropriate artifacts such as AGENTS.md/CONTEXT.md/ADR/skills/hooks, avoids generic notes, and verifies changes.
assertions: The output extracts concrete reusable project learnings rather than generic advice.
The output chooses an appropriate destination such as AGENTS.md, CONTEXT.md, ADRs, skills, commands, or hooks.
The output preserves project domain language or decisions from the session.
The output avoids saving secrets or transient details as permanent instructions.
The output includes verification or review of the generated customization.

evals.name: boundary doc update
evals.prompt: A user says: update the README for the new CLI flag. Describe how learning-patterns should respond.
evals.expected_output: The response identifies this as documentation update, not learning extraction, and routes to documenting-code unless there is a reusable instruction pattern to capture.
assertions: The output says a README update is primarily a documentation task.
The output routes to documenting-code or a docs workflow.
The output does not create project instruction customizations unless there is a reusable pattern to save.
The output asks or notes whether any durable learning should also be captured.
